# Architecture Requirements Quality Checklist: Sirva CMS Marketplace Platform

**Purpose**: Validate whether platform architecture requirements are complete, clear, consistent, and measurable before implementation/review.
**Created**: 2026-03-13
**Feature**: `specs/main/plan.md`, `specs/main/tasks.md`, `specs/001-sirva-cms-marketplace/spec.md`

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are Angular project structure requirements explicitly defined for all mandatory root and app folders (including `core`, `shared`, `features`, and `models`)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-020, Plan §Project Structure]
- [ ] CHK002 Are required feature modules and their bounded responsibilities fully specified (dashboard, vendors, widgets, landing pages, rules, analytics, AI)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-020, Spec §AR-001..AR-007]
- [ ] CHK003 Are shared module responsibilities explicitly documented as reusable UI capabilities, distinct from core responsibilities? [Completeness, Spec §AR-008, Plan §Reusable UI Components]
- [ ] CHK004 Are core module responsibilities explicitly documented to include API communication/services and cross-cutting platform concerns? [Completeness, Spec §AR-008, Plan §API Communication Layer]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK005 Is the required organization of feature modules specific enough to avoid ambiguity about folder boundaries, routing ownership, and cross-feature dependencies? [Clarity, Plan §Project Structure, Plan §Routing Architecture]
- [ ] CHK006 Is "routing configuration works correctly" translated into explicit routing requirements (default route, lazy loading, route ownership, and expected route patterns per module)? [Clarity, Plan §Routing Architecture, Tasks §T032]
- [ ] CHK007 Are accessibility requirements defined with actionable criteria for UI components (for example keyboard operation, focus behavior, semantics, and labels) rather than only high-level WCAG statements? [Clarity, Spec §FR-019, Spec §SC-008, Gap]
- [ ] CHK008 Are "supports scalability for additional modules" expectations quantified with architectural constraints (module isolation, extension points, and onboarding pattern for new domains)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-022, Plan §Modular Scalability]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK009 Do architecture requirements remain consistent across spec, plan, and tasks regarding the `core` vs `shared` split and API service placement? [Consistency, Spec §AR-008, Plan §Structure Decision, Tasks §T004/T011/T024]
- [ ] CHK010 Are feature module names and route segments consistent across planning and task definitions (for example `landing-pages`, `rules`, and `ai`)? [Consistency, Plan §Routing Architecture, Tasks §T032]
- [ ] CHK011 Are reusable component ownership expectations consistent between shared module requirements and feature module boundaries? [Consistency, Spec §FR-018, Spec §AR-008, Plan §Reusable UI Components]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK012 Can conformance to project structure requirements be objectively assessed from written acceptance criteria (not implied conventions)? [Measurability, Spec §FR-020, Gap]
- [ ] CHK013 Are acceptance criteria for API-call UI states measurable and complete for loading, empty, success, and error behaviors including retry expectations? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §FR-031]
- [ ] CHK014 Are routing acceptance criteria measurable for successful lazy-loaded module entry and fallback behavior when a route segment is unavailable? [Measurability, Plan §Routing Architecture, Gap]

## Scenario and Edge Coverage

- [ ] CHK015 Are exception and recovery requirements defined for API failures at module and shared-component levels (including degraded mode and user guidance)? [Coverage, Exception Flow, Spec §FR-031]
- [ ] CHK016 Are scalability scenario requirements defined for adding a new module (required artifacts, routing registration, shared/core usage rules, and dependency assumptions)? [Coverage, Recovery/Change Flow, Spec §FR-022, Plan §Modular Scalability]
- [ ] CHK017 Are non-functional accessibility requirements consistently applied across all core workflows and reusable components, not only module pages? [Non-Functional, Spec §FR-019, Spec §SC-008]

## Dependencies and Assumptions

- [ ] CHK018 Are assumptions about API contract stability, versioning, and centralized service orchestration explicitly documented for architecture decisions? [Assumption, Dependency, Spec §FR-021, Spec §FR-025, Spec §FR-026]
- [ ] CHK019 Is the dependency on Angular Router lazy loading and module encapsulation explicitly stated as a requirement, not only an implementation preference? [Dependency, Plan §Routing Architecture, Plan §Modular Scalability, Ambiguity]

## Ambiguities and Conflicts

- [ ] CHK020 Do any requirements conflict between "shared reusable components" and "feature autonomy" when deciding where new UI elements should live? [Conflict, Spec §FR-018, Spec §AR-008]
- [ ] CHK021 Are terms such as "correctly organized", "works correctly", and "supports scalability" replaced or augmented with explicit, testable requirement language? [Ambiguity, Gap]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
